channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: D-backs, SS Geraldo Perdomo agree to $45 million, 4-year deal that starts in 2026, AP source says
item.description: Slick-fielding shortstop Geraldo Perdomo has agreed to a $45 million, four-year contract with the Arizona Diamondbacks that begins in 2026, a person familiar with the deal told The Associated Press on Monday.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Devers insists he's the Red Sox's third baseman even with Bregman now on the team
item.description: FORT MYERS, Fla. (AP) — The Red Sox's ballyhooed signing of Alex Bregman was met with considerable resistance Monday when Rafael Devers said he wouldn't cede third base.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kotsay signs new deal that could keep him with A’s through 2029
item.description: Manager Mark Kotsay helped the Athletics navigate three difficult season while the franchise lost a lot of games, built a young roster and dealt with an emotional departure from Oakland.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Canadian national anthem booed, but only lightly, at 4 Nations hockey tournament
item.description: The Canadian national anthem was lightly booed by the fans at the 4 Nations Face-Off on Monday in apparent payback for the jeering of “The Star-Spangled Banner” during the opening rounds in Montreal last week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Auburn stays at No. 1 in AP Top 25 for 6th straight week, Florida up to No. 2; Louisville returns
item.description: Auburn's win at Alabama assured the Tigers would keep the No. 1 ranking in The Associated Press Top 25 men's college basketball poll for a sixth consecutive week.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Notre Dame basketball coach goes on fiery rant in defense of team after latest loss
item.description: Notre Dame Fighting Irish men's basketball head coach Micah Shrewsberry had a fiery defense of his team following a loss to Louisville on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: William Byron speaks to NewsNation after Daytona 500 win
item.description: Byron made sure to celebrate his victory, sleeping for an hour before returning to Daytona International Speedway on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Yankees' Stanton hasn't swung bat in 3 or 4 weeks and is unsure of whether he can play in opener
item.description: New York Yankees slugger Giancarlo Stanton hasn’t swung a bat in three or four weeks and isn’t sure whether he will be ready for opening day because of painful tendinitis in both elbows.
item.dc:creator: Ronald Blum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The idea of a US vs. World matchup in the NBA All-Star Game has some player support
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Victor Wembanyama has already gone up against the U.S. in international events, most notably last year's Paris Olympics when he led France's march to the gold medal game against the Americans. He'd like to be in that position more often — maybe even annually.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shaw performs Black Power salute after goal for Man City Women following racist abuse
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Khadija ‘Bunny’ Shaw bowed her head and raised a gloved fist after scoring a goal on her first start for Manchester City Women since pulling out of the team because of racist abuse directed at her on social media.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UCLA, South Carolina, Texas and Notre Dame are No. 1 seeded teams in first NCAA Tournament reveal
item.description: UCLA, South Carolina, Texas and Notre Dame would be the No. 1 seeds in the women's NCAA Tournament if it began now. The NCAA basketball selection committee on Sunday did its first reveal of the teams in line for the top 16 seeds.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-Dolphins lineman Jonathan Martin walks back bullying allegations against teammate that caused NFL scandal
item.description: In 2013, ex-Miami Dolphins lineman Jonathan Martin accused teammate Richie Incognito of bullying. 12 years later, he's walking back those comments that led to NFL scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tiger Woods expects golf to 'heal quickly' when PGA Tour gets deal with Saudis
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Tiger Woods expects the fractured landscape of golf to “heal quickly” when the PGA Tour and the Saudi backers of rival LIV Golf reach an agreement that appears to be imminent.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ludvig Aberg rallies down the stretch at Torrey Pines to win the Genesis Invitational
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Ludvig Aberg never felt worse leaving a golf course than three weeks ago at Torrey Pines when he shared the 36-hole lead only to get violently ill from a stomach ailment and stagger to the finish with a 79.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Patrick Rodgers takes the lead at Torrey Pines as Scheffler and McIlroy falter
item.description: Rodgers was at his best Saturday when golf's best were faltering on a tough day at Torrey Pines, playing mistake-free until dumping a gap wedge into the water on the par-5 18th for his only bogey. That gave him a 4-under 68 and a one-shot lead going into the final round.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Boys Basketball: Montgomery 58, Victory Christian Academy 44
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Boys hoops in Chula Vista on Tuesday night with Montgomery defeating Victory Christian Academy 58-44. Aztecs were lead by Senior JJ Sanchez. Montgomery was down at half and got going in the 2nd half for the double digit win.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Utah Warriors kick off 2025 season next week
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - About to begin its eighth season in Major League Rugby, the Utah Warriors are out to improve upon last year's 5-11 record. "I think we do really well this year," said fullback Sione Mahe.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Muni will be free for NBA-All Star, Lunar New Year weekend, SFMTA announces
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- With thousands of people expected to descend on San Francisco this weekend for the NBA All-Stars and Lunar New Year Parade, officials are urging people to leave their cars at home.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Countdown to the 2025 Daytona 500: Drivers get ready for season opener
item.description: The biggest race in NASCAR is only a few days away, where 40 drivers will compete to win “The Great American Race.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz turn the tables on Lakers in 131-119 victory
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - What a difference 48 hours make. On Monday, the L.A. Lakers blew away the Utah Jazz in Luka Doncic' Laker debut. But Wednesday in Salt Lake City, it was all Jazz.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Oakland high school gym named after NBA legend, alumnus Bill Russell set to open
item.description: (KRON/BCN) -- The Oakland Unified School District will hold a ribbon-cutting ceremony on Wednesday for a new school gymnasium at McClymonds High School named after late NBA legend Bill Russell, who was an alumnus of the school. The ceremony will take place at 3:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Girls Soccer: University City 4, Madison 0
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)-Girls soccer University City beat Madison 4-0 on senior night for the Warhawks. Sofia Christensen scored twice for the Centurions.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: NBA All-Star 2025 tip-off party canceled due to weather
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The official NBA All-Star 2025 tip-off party, which was set to take place Thursday at Thrive City has been canceled due to weather. The event was due to take place between 5 p.m. and 9 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4
